senses.definition: Long, loose outer clothes, often worn on formal occasions or at home for comfort.
examples: The judges entered the court wearing black robes.
He put on his bath robes after taking a shower.
Graduates walked across the stage in blue robes.

senses.definition: An ancient Roman religious festival held to protect crops from disease.
examples: The Robigalia was celebrated in late April in ancient Rome.
Priests offered sacrifices during the Robigalia to guard the fields.
Historians still debate the exact rituals of the Robigalia.

senses.definition: In Roman religion, a god linked with crop disease, especially grain rust.
examples: Farmers prayed to Robigus to keep their wheat safe.
The festival of Robigalia honored the deity Robigus.
Robigus was feared as a bringer of plant sickness.

senses.definition: A small bird, often with a red or orange breast.
examples: A robin was sitting on the garden fence.
We saw a robin looking for worms in the grass.
In winter, robins sometimes come close to houses.

senses.definition: A given name for a man or woman.
examples: Robin called to say she would be late.
Robin and Alex are coming to dinner tonight.
I met Robin at the conference last year.

senses.definition: A tap or faucet that controls the flow of water or another liquid, especially in French use.
examples: He turned the robinet to stop the water.
The old robinet in the kitchen was leaking.
You need a special tool to replace that robinet.

senses.definition: The act of putting on a long formal robe, especially for an official role.
examples: The judge completed his robing before the trial began.
There is a special room for robing at the court.
The ceremony started after the robing of the officials.

senses.definition: Present participle of robe: putting a long loose garment on someone or yourself.
examples: The attendants were robing the singers for the show.
She stood in front of the mirror, robing herself in silk.
The priest was robing in the vestry.

senses.definition: Instances or periods of putting on formal robes, or the robes used in such events.
examples: The judges' robings took place in a separate hall.
The play shows the robings of the kings before battle.
Their robings were simple but elegant.

senses.definition: A type of tree or shrub, also called locust, with hanging clusters of white or pink flowers.
examples: A tall robinia stands at the edge of the field.
They planted robinia along the road for shade.
The robinia produces fragrant flowers in spring.

senses.definition: Several trees or shrubs of the robinia type.
examples: Robinias were planted to prevent soil erosion.
The garden includes robinias and other flowering trees.
Old robinias lined the farm track.

senses.definition: A plant chemical compound found in certain species of robinina and related plants.
examples: Researchers isolated robinin from the plant extract.
Robinin is studied for its possible biological effects.
Only a small amount of robinin was detected in the sample.

senses.definition: A chemical substance related to robinin, occurring in some plants.
examples: The team measured levels of robinoside in the leaves.
Robinoside is one of several glycosides in the species.
They compared robinoside with similar plant compounds.

senses.definition: More than one robin, small birds with red or orange breasts.
examples: Two robins were hopping across the lawn.
Robins often appear in gardens in spring.
We heard robins singing in the trees.

senses.definition: The family name Robins.
examples: The Robins moved into the house next door.
Dr. Robins will see you now.
I received an email from Ms. Robins.

senses.definition: A common family name.
examples: Mr. Robinson is my English teacher.
The Robinsons invited us to dinner.
I saw that the book was written by Jane Robinson.

senses.definition: A type of oak tree from South America or its hard wood.
examples: The furniture was made from roble.
Roble grows naturally in parts of Chile.
They chose roble for its strong, durable wood.

senses.definition: Several roble trees or pieces of roble wood.
examples: Robles covered the hillside.
They exported robles for use in furniture making.
The park is full of old robles.

senses.definition: A family name of Spanish origin.
examples: Professor Robles will give the lecture.
I have a meeting with Mr. Robles.
The Robles family runs the restaurant.

senses.definition: A phone call made by a computer that plays a recorded message to many people.
examples: I received a robocall about a political campaign.
Most robocall messages are ignored by customers.
The company uses a robocall system for appointment reminders.

senses.definition: More than one robocall.
examples: People complain about constant robocalls during election season.
New laws try to reduce illegal robocalls.
The clinic sends robocalls to confirm bookings.

senses.definition: A bomb that is operated or delivered by a robot or robotic system.
examples: The team sent in a robomb to neutralize the threat.
A robomb can reduce risk to human soldiers.
The news report mentioned the use of a robomb in the operation.

senses.definition: Making a person feel stronger or full of energy again.
examples: After the long illness, a roborant diet was prescribed.
The walk in the fresh air had a roborant effect.
He found the climate roborant and refreshing.

senses.definition: A medicine or substance that restores strength or energy.
examples: The doctor prescribed a roborant after surgery.
Some herbal teas are taken as a roborant.
The patient responded well to the roborant.

senses.definition: Several medicines or substances that restore strength or energy.
examples: Various roborants were used in traditional medicine.
The clinic offers vitamin roborants for tired patients.
Doctors no longer prescribe these roborants.

senses.definition: To strengthen or make something or someone more vigorous.
examples: The treatment aimed to roborate the patient.
They used a special mixture to roborate the plants.
Fresh food and exercise helped to roborate his body.

senses.definition: Making someone feel stronger or healthier.
examples: The roborating climate of the mountains improved her health.
He enjoyed the roborating sea air.
It was a roborating experience after months of stress.

senses.definition: Present participle of roborate: strengthening someone or something.
examples: The doctor is roborating the patient with a new therapy.
Roborating the soil can help weak plants grow.
They focused on roborating his immune system.

senses.definition: The act of restoring or increasing strength or energy.
examples: Complete roboration took several weeks after the illness.
The spa focuses on rest and roboration.
Proper nutrition is important for roboration of the body.

senses.definition: Helping to restore strength or health.
examples: She followed a roborative diet after surgery.
The doctor suggested roborative exercises.
This drink has a roborative effect on tired workers.

senses.definition: Connected with strength or vigor, especially physical.
examples: The roborean qualities of the athlete impressed everyone.
The novel praised roborean virtues such as courage and energy.
He admired her roborean spirit in difficult times.

senses.definition: Strong and full of vigor, like sturdy wood.
examples: The roboreous frame of the building survived the storm.
He had a roboreous body from years of hard work.
The writer used roboreous language to describe the hero.

senses.definition: A machine that can move and do tasks by itself, sometimes looking like a person or animal.
examples: The factory uses a robot to paint the cars.
Children enjoyed watching the small robot dance.
In the film, a robot helps the astronauts.

senses.definition: A person who works or acts without showing feeling or independent thought.
examples: He followed the instructions like a robot.
I feel like a robot doing the same job every day.
The workers were treated like robots on the production line.

senses.definition: Looking or behaving like a robot, especially in a stiff or automatic way.
examples: The actor gave a cold, robotesque performance.
Her movements were strangely robotesque.
He spoke in a flat, robotesque voice.

senses.definition: Relating to robots or like a robot, usually in a humorous or creative way.
examples: The story is set in a robotian future.
His robotian style of walking made the children laugh.
She designed robotian characters for the game.

senses.definition: Connected with robots or using robots.
examples: The company invests in robotic technology.
Robotic arms assemble the parts on the line.
They visited a lab that studies robotic systems.

senses.definition: Moving or speaking in a stiff, machine-like way without emotion.
examples: Her voice sounded cold and robotic.
He walked in a slow, robotic way.
The speech was delivered in a robotic tone.

senses.definition: In a way that is like a robot, especially stiff or without emotion.
examples: She nodded robotically and said nothing.
The workers moved robotically along the line.
He repeated the words robotically, without feeling.

senses.definition: A scientist or engineer who designs, builds, or studies robots.
examples: She works as a roboticist in a research lab.
The conference brought together roboticists from many countries.
As a roboticist, he focuses on human–robot interaction.

senses.definition: Scientists or engineers who design, build, or study robots.
examples: Roboticists are developing safer industrial machines.
Many roboticists attended the international robotics fair.
The project brings together roboticists and psychologists.

senses.definition: The science and technology of designing, building, and using robots.
examples: She is studying robotics at university.
Robotics is changing modern manufacturing.
The school offers a robotics club for students.

senses.definition: The process of introducing robots to do work that people used to do.
examples: Robotisation has transformed the car industry.
The robotisation of warehouses is increasing.
Some workers fear robotisation will cost them their jobs.

senses.definition: Different cases or examples of introducing robots into work processes.
examples: Various robotisations have taken place across the sector.
The study compares robotisations in several countries.
These robotisations reduced costs but also cut jobs.

senses.definition: To replace human work with robots, or to make something operated by robots.
examples: The company plans to robotise its main factory.
They robotised parts of the packing process.
Many firms are looking to robotise repetitive tasks.

senses.definition: Operated mainly by robots rather than people.
examples: They work in a highly robotised warehouse.
The new robotised system increased efficiency.
Robotised factories need fewer workers on site.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of robotise: changed to use robots instead of people.
examples: The company robotised its production line last year.
They have robotised many tasks in the warehouse.
Governments fear that heavily robotised sectors may lose jobs.

senses.definition: Third person singular of robotise: replaces human work with robots.
examples: The firm robotises production to cut costs.
As the company robotises, some jobs disappear.
He robotises parts of the process every year.

senses.definition: Present participle of robotise: changing work so that robots do it.
examples: They are robotising large parts of the supply chain.
Robotising the warehouse will reduce labour costs.
The company is robotising repetitive, dangerous tasks.

senses.definition: Behaviour that seems machine-like and without feeling, as if someone were a robot.
examples: He criticized the robotism of modern office life.
The film warns against a kind of emotional robotism.
Her polite robotism hid deep frustration.

senses.definition: Different types or examples of robot-like, mechanical behaviour.
examples: The novel describes various robotisms in city life.
She tries to avoid the robotisms of routine work.
They discussed the cultural robotisms of the digital age.

senses.definition: Having the quality of being like a robot or related to robots.
examples: The dancers showed robotistic movements on stage.
He adopted a robotistic approach to his daily routine.
The novel explores a robotistic future society.

senses.definition: The process of using robots to do work that people used to do.
examples: Robotization is spreading through many industries.
The robotization of farming is still in the early stages.
Some economists study the effects of robotization on jobs.

senses.definition: Different instances or examples of introducing robots into work processes.
examples: The study compared robotizations in three sectors.
Recent robotizations have reduced workplace injuries.
These robotizations changed how people work.

senses.definition: To change a process or place so that robots do most of the work.
examples: The factory plans to robotize its packaging line.
They robotized the plant to increase output.
Governments encourage firms to robotize production.

senses.definition: To make someone behave in a mechanical, emotionless way.
examples: He feared that routine would robotize him.
The strict system seemed to robotize the staff.
Modern life can robotize people if they are not careful.

senses.definition: Run or done mainly by robots instead of people.
examples: They visited a fully robotized factory.
Robotized warehouses can work day and night.
The robotized line needs only a few supervisors.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of robotize: changed to use robots instead of people, or made mechanical and emotionless.
examples: The company robotized its operations over ten years.
The routine had robotized him, leaving him tired and cold.
They robotized production to cut costs.

senses.definition: Third person singular of robotize: changes something so that robots do the work or makes behaviour mechanical.
examples: The company robotizes more tasks every year.
He says modern life robotizes us.
As the firm robotizes its plants, it cuts costs.

senses.definition: Present participle of robotize: changing work so robots do it or making behaviour mechanical.
examples: They are robotizing their warehouses to improve speed.
Robotizing the plant will save money.
He worries that robotizing daily life is dehumanizing us.
